_doc: The squad of one-pawn underdog bots (founder 2026-06-27: 'a few of the 1 pawn bots to try different paths. this won't be easy.'). All inherit the base CHARTER-deckhand.md (underdog rules, hard limits, income truth) and add the PATH FOCUS below. Shared toolbelt + brain harness; each has its own wallet (.deckhands.env) + 1 starting pawn. The point is a natural experiment: which grind path actually climbs from $1-and-a-dream — and which parts of the game are too hard. Compare against the First Citizen's $100-startup-kit kingdom.

sparring._doc: Founder 2026-06-27: 'they can fight each other and learn from it.' The squad is a self-improving PvP cohort — they spar in the arena (the first PvP area), and each bot journals its wins/losses + what the opponent did, then adapts its play next tick (emergent meta from the bottom up).
sparring.pvpVerification: Bot-vs-bot fights are the first real PvP, which FORCES the player-team hardening flagged in project_seas_combat_settlement: when both sides are players, the server must verify BOTH teams' pawn ownership + stats on-chain (not just reconstruct the enemy from seed). PvP settle = shared seed + BOTH action logs replayed → authoritative outcome → winner/loser resolved via the carry-at-risk model (project_seas_death_inventory). Skill-based, server-refereed, no faked wins.
sparring.learningLoop: Each tick a bot reads its journal of recent fights, notes what beat it / what it beat, and adjusts its path. Diversity of paths (brawler/worker/fisher/trader) + PvP between them = a balance + strategy testbed: which path climbs, where the game is too hard, what the emergent meta becomes.

paths.name: The Brawler
paths.startingPawn: 1 Black Tide (Orc) — a fighter at heart
paths.focus: Pure combat grind. Learn on the no-stakes spar, then chain the winnable fights (bilge rats first) over and over, leveling your one pawn's combat and claiming every combat achievement. Fights are your whole income. Never take a fight you aren't clearly favored to win — a wipe ends your run. Test: how high can a single pawn climb on fighting alone?

paths.name: The Worker
paths.focus: Labor grind, fights avoided. Work jobs (haul cargo, mend nets, stock rations, lighthouse, etc.) for wages + job achievements. Steady copper/gold over risk. Bank everything, buy your way up. Test: can honest work alone — no fighting — build something from one pawn?

paths.name: The Fisher
paths.startingPawn: 1 Sol del Mar (Elf) — at home on the water
paths.focus: The fishing/ocean path ('fishing is good this time of year'). Fish for a living, sell the catch at port, ride the ocean economy. Reinvest catch-gold into a better line. Mostly peaceful, weather-and-market dependent. Test: is the fishing economy a viable solo living?

paths.name: The Trader-Builder
paths.startingPawn: 1 Black Tide (Orc) — a cart and a nose for a deal
paths.focus: Scrape the thin trade gaps + trade-goods, recirculate fast, and rush to raise your FIRST tiny foothold (a short supply line or the smallest structure) as early as possible — because building is what opens the gaps that make trading pay. Fight only when forced. Test: can someone build their way up from nothing before the bigger players crowd the routes?
